parameters-en.Decay: Sets the decay of the reverb (0.1 sec ~ 45.0 sec, or Infinity). TIP: Assign a second stomp switch to toggle between a lower Decay value and Infinity. Label it "ForEVER ever?"
parameters-en.Predelay: Controls the amount of delay heard before the audible onset of reverb. Can sometimes result in more definition between the dry and effected signals.
parameters-en.Room Size: Sets the size of the hall (10, 20, or 30 meters). NOTE: This parameter actually changes the algorithm so you'll hear a small bump when changing it. Therefore, we don't recommend assigning Room Size to snapshots or other controllers.
parameters-en.Diffusion: Sets the amount of smearing between discrete echoes, sometimes resulting in a softer effected signal.
parameters-en.Damping: Determines the frequency above which the reverb will be absorbed. For example, if your hall is full of people wearing inflatable sumo wrestler Halloween costumes, more high frequencies would be absorbed than if the room were empty.
parameters-en.Mix: Controls the blend between the reverb and the dry signal. At 0%, no reverb is heard; at 100%, no dry signal is heard.
parameters-en.Motion: Sets the amount of randomization, which can be helpful to minimize any metallic artifacts common in static reverbs. At higher values, can impart a bit of modulation to the effected signal.
parameters-en.Low Freq: Sets the frequency below which the Low Gain parameter is applied.
parameters-en.Low Gain: Controls the reverb time for frequencies below the Low Freq value. Values below 0.0dB mean the bass frequencies decay faster than the treble frequencies; values above 0.0dB mean the bass frequencies decay slower than the treble frequencies.
parameters-en.Low Cut: Applies a low cut (or high pass) filter to the reverb, letting you remove the effected signal below a certain frequency.
parameters-en.High Cut: Applies a high cut (or low pass) filter to the reverb, letting you remove the effected signal above a certain frequency.
parameters-en.Level: Sets the overall level of the block.
